title: Breadcrumb, Sidebar & Menu
intro: Three navigation surfaces that answer 'where am I' and 'what can I do here'. `wf-breadcrumb` shows hierarchical position in a deep tree, `wf-sidebar` is the persistent top-level app rail that always marks your current location, and `wf-menu` is the floating dropdown / context menu for grouped actions. Each leans on the neutral scale and reserves `--wf-color-primary` for the one element that says 'you are here'.

blocks.body:
Pick the surface by the question the user is asking. `wf-breadcrumb` answers 'where am I in the hierarchy' — it shows your position *and* the parents, so reach for it only inside a deep tree (Contracts / Active / CNT-2024-001). Per the DESIGN.md navigation rule, a one-level breadcrumb is just a heading — don't render it.

`wf-sidebar` is the persistent top-level rail. It's the user's primary mental model for 'where am I in the app', it survives across screens, and exactly one `wf-sidebar-item` carries `wf-sidebar-item-active` to mark the current location. `wf-menu` is the opposite of persistent — a floating dropdown anchored to a trigger inside `wf-menu-container`, holding grouped actions (Edit, Duplicate, Delete) or a context menu off a `⋮` button.

All three are pure HTML/CSS. There are no props — you compose state by adding modifier classes (`wf-breadcrumb-current`, `wf-sidebar-item-active`, `wf-menu-item-danger`) to the canonical markup. Copy the markup verbatim; don't invent class names.

blocks.title: Breadcrumb in a page header
blocks.caption: The trail sits above the page title. Parent links (Contracts, Active) render muted in `--wf-color-text-muted`; the leaf gets `wf-breadcrumb-current` — darker `--wf-color-text` at 500 weight, and it is a plain span, not a link.

blocks.title: Breadcrumb parts
blocks.intro: A `wf-breadcrumb` is a flat row of items and separators inside a <nav>. Links are <a>; the current leaf is a non-link <span>. The separator glyph is literal text — swap '/' for '›' or '→' as you like.

rows.name: wf-breadcrumb-item
rows.when: A parent crumb. As an <a>, it's a muted link that turns `--wf-color-primary` and underlines on hover.

rows.name: wf-breadcrumb-current
rows.when: The active leaf — your current location. Pair on a <span>, never an <a>: it's where you already are.

rows.name: wf-breadcrumb-separator
rows.when: The divider between crumbs. Holds the literal glyph and is `user-select: none` so it won't copy.

rows.name: wf-breadcrumb-icon
rows.when: Add to an item to inline a leading glyph with the label, with a 4px gap.

rows.name: wf-breadcrumb-ellipsis
rows.when: Collapse a deep middle. A 24x24 clickable token standing in for hidden ancestors between the root and the leaf.

blocks.title: Collapsed breadcrumb (ellipsis)
blocks.caption: For deep paths, the middle ancestors collapse to a single `wf-breadcrumb-ellipsis` token between the root and the active leaf — keeping the trail one line without losing the start/end anchors.

blocks.title: Breadcrumb trail
blocks.code: Home / Contracts / CNT-2024-001
blocks.note: The separator carries the glyph as text — change '/' to '›' or '→' to restyle the whole trail without touching CSS.

blocks.title: Sidebar with nested navigation
blocks.caption: The nav rail: Contracts carries `wf-sidebar-item-active` (solid `--wf-color-primary` fill, inverse text), its children sit in a `wf-sidebar-nested` block with a left border, and Pending Review carries a `wf-badge wf-badge-sm` count.

blocks.title: Sidebar parts
blocks.intro: A `wf-sidebar` is an <aside> stacking a header, a scrollable `wf-sidebar-nav`, and a footer. Items are <a>; one — and only one — gets the active modifier.

rows.name: wf-sidebar-item
rows.when: A nav link. Muted resting; on hover it lifts to `--wf-color-bg-hover` with `--wf-color-text`.

rows.name: wf-sidebar-item-active
rows.when: The current location — exactly one per rail. Solid `--wf-color-primary` fill with inverse text; hover deepens to `--wf-color-primary-hover`.

rows.name: wf-sidebar-nested
rows.when: Second-level children under a parent item. Indents and draws a left border to show the tree relationship.
rows.code: All Contracts Pending Review3

rows.name: wf-sidebar-group + wf-sidebar-group-label
rows.when: Cluster related items under an uppercased section header (e.g. Settings).

rows.name: wf-sidebar-divider
rows.when: A 1px rule separating clusters within the nav.

rows.name: wf-sidebar-collapsed
rows.when: Icons-only rail. Add to the root <aside>: width drops to 64px and labels, group labels, and badges hide. Add a `title` to each item so the icon stays labelled.

rows.name: wf-sidebar-dark
rows.when: Dark theme. Add to the root <aside>: gray-900 background, items in gray-400 text. The active item keeps the same `--wf-color-primary` fill.

blocks.title: Sidebar rail
blocks.code:
Gravitate
📊 Dashboard 📋 Contracts 5
Settings Profile
User info / logout
blocks.note: `wf-sidebar-label` is the flex-growing text that gets truncated and hidden when collapsed; badges follow it and also hide when collapsed.

blocks.title: Action / context menu
blocks.caption: A `wf-menu` dropdown in context-menu style off a `⋮` trigger: grouped actions with hover affordance, a `wf-menu-divider` before the destructive item, and Delete styled with `wf-menu-item-danger` in `--wf-color-danger`.

blocks.title: Menu parts
blocks.intro: A `wf-menu` is an absolutely-positioned dropdown anchored inside a relative `wf-menu-container`. Items are <button>s for keyboard operability. It opens below-left by default.

rows.name: wf-menu-container
rows.when: The relative anchor wrapping the trigger and the menu. The menu positions against it.

rows.name: wf-menu-item
rows.when: A standard action row. A full-width left-aligned <button> that highlights to `--wf-color-bg-hover` on hover and focus.

rows.name: wf-menu-item-danger
rows.when: A destructive action. Renders in `--wf-color-danger` with a tinted `--wf-color-error-bg` hover.

rows.name: disabled (attribute)
rows.when: An unavailable action. The native disabled attribute mutes the text to `--wf-color-text-disabled` and sets not-allowed.

rows.name: wf-menu-divider
rows.when: A 1px rule separating action clusters — typically before a destructive item.

rows.name: wf-menu-label
rows.when: An uppercased section header inside the menu (Display, Sort By).

rows.name: wf-menu-shortcut
rows.when: A keyboard hint pushed to the right edge of an item via margin-left:auto.

rows.name: wf-menu-item-check / -checked
rows.when: A toggleable item. -check reserves a left checkmark slot; add -checked to show the ✓ in `--wf-color-primary`.

rows.name: wf-menu-right
rows.when: Right-align the dropdown to its trigger (account/profile menus near the right edge).

blocks.title: Context menu with a destructive action
blocks.note: Items are real <button>s, so they're tab-reachable and show the same `--wf-color-bg-hover` highlight on focus as on hover.

blocks.title: Tokens these components lean on
blocks.intro: Every value here comes from a token. The fallbacks shown are the literals baked into navigation.css.

rows.use: The single 'you are here' signal: active sidebar item fill, breadcrumb link hover, menu checkmark.
Deepened fill when hovering the active sidebar item.
Resting breadcrumb links and resting sidebar items.
Breadcrumb current leaf and standard menu item text.
Label on the active (primary-filled) sidebar item.
Breadcrumb separators, disabled menu items, menu shortcuts.
Hover/focus surface on sidebar items and menu items.
Hover surface behind a destructive menu item.
Sidebar right border, dividers, menu border, nested left rule.
Stacking layer for the open menu.

blocks.title: When to use which
blocks.intro: From the DESIGN.md navigation decision tree (§4.4): peer views → Tabs; ordered path → Stepper; top-level location → Sidebar; hierarchy crumbs → Breadcrumb.

items.rule: Use the breadcrumb only inside a deep hierarchy where the parents matter.
items.why: Its job is to show position AND ancestors in a tree like Contracts / 2026 / ACME / Schedule.
The sidebar is the user's mental model for 'where am I in the app' — two active items means two answers to one question.

items.rule: Reserve the primary fill for the active item only; everything else is neutral.
items.why: Per the color-intent rules, primary marks the current location and interactive intent — not decoration.

items.rule: Reach for a Menu for actions, not for navigation between views.
items.why: Switching peer views is Tabs; an ordered flow is a Stepper. A dropdown is for verbs (Edit, Export, Delete).

items.rule: Put a wf-menu-divider before any destructive item, and style it wf-menu-item-danger.
items.why: Color plus separation plus the trash glyph is the safety signal — color alone is never enough.
The current leaf is where you already are. Only the hover (link) treatment lives on a.wf-breadcrumb-item, so a current <a> invites a click to nowhere.
DESIGN.md §7.5: a single-level breadcrumb is just a heading — render a heading (the page-header demo pairs the trail with an <h2>) instead.
The active fill is the location signal; multiple lit items destroy the 'where am I' answer.

items.do: Add a title to each item when wf-sidebar-collapsed
items.dont: Collapse the rail and let bare icons stand alone
items.why: Collapsed hides wf-sidebar-label, so the title attribute is the only thing left labelling the glyph for hover and screen readers.
The wf-menu-item focus style and keyboard reachability depend on a real button; a div is invisible to Tab.

items.title: The breadcrumb hover only applies to links
items.detail: The hover rule is scoped to `a.wf-breadcrumb-item` — color shift to primary plus underline. A current <span> correctly gets no hover, which is why the leaf must be a span, not a styled link.

items.title: Collapsing the sidebar hides labels, group labels, and badges
items.detail: wf-sidebar-collapsed sets `display:none` on wf-sidebar-label, wf-sidebar-group-label, and any .wf-badge inside an item. The icon and the title attribute are all that remain — so a badge count you were relying on disappears.

items.title: Nested items drop their icon by convention
items.detail: In the source, wf-sidebar-nested children carry only a wf-sidebar-label (no wf-sidebar-icon). The left border and indent of wf-sidebar-nested carry the hierarchy instead of a per-item glyph.

items.title: The menu is hidden by positioning, not by a state class
items.detail: wf-menu is absolutely positioned at top:100% under its trigger inside wf-menu-container; it has no built-in open/closed class. The demo forces it visible with a static-position override — wiring the open/close toggle is on you.

items.title: wf-menu-item-check reserves space whether or not it's checked
items.detail: The -check modifier adds left padding and a 16px ::before slot so checked and unchecked items align. The ✓ only appears when you also add wf-menu-item-checked; -check alone is an empty (unchecked) slot.
